title: Playing Esoteric Ebb is like rolling the dice with a great DM
summary: Esoteric Ebb is a new CRPG combining Disco Elysium's dialogue mechanics with Dungeons & Dragons fantasy aesthetics. Players control a cleric unraveling a tea shop explosion while managing competing voices in their mind through reading-heavy gameplay. The game emphasizes decision-making and chance rolls in an isometric perspective.
content:
It took me a while to get into Esoteric Ebb, a new CRPG from developer Christoffer Bodegård. The elevator pitch is basically Disco Elysium, but in the fantasy style of Dungeons & Dragons: You play as a cleric wandering around a small town who's trying to figure out, among other things, the mystery of why a tea shop in town exploded, and all the while, you're having conversations with different character traits in your head. Like Disco Elysium, you see the world from an isometric, top-down perspective. Also like Disco Elysium, Esoteric Ebb requires a lot of reading, weighing the opinions of your competing voices, and making some bold dice rol …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 玩Esoteric Ebb就像和伟大的地下城主掷骰子
zh.summary: Esoteric Ebb是一款结合《Disco Elysium》对话机制和龙与地下城幻想美学的新型CRPG。玩家控制一位牧师在解开茶馆爆炸事件的同时，通过阅读密集的玩法管理心中的竞争声音。游戏强调等距视角下的决策制定和概率骰子。

title: The things we’re building
summary: The Verge's Installer newsletter (issue 126) curates recent discoveries and recommendations from the publication's team members' interests. This edition showcases diverse items including podcasts, streaming shows, design products like Japanese stationery, and tech innovations.
content:
Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 126, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, I need 10 or 15 skirts from Calvin Klein, and also you can read all the old editions at the Installer homepage.) 
Happy Ruthless Self-Promotion Week! We're dedicating almost all of this issue to the stuff we've been making recently. Personally, I've been reading about the Tesla diner and Dwarkesh Patel and The Rest Is History, starting a Ted Lasso rewatch to get ready for season 4, watching a robot injure Joanna Stern, continuing down the rabbit hole of gorgeous Japanese stationery, wondering if those cool shoes …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 我们在构建的事物
zh.summary: The Verge的Installer通讯（第126期）精选该媒体团队成员兴趣中的最新发现和推荐。本期展示了各种项目，包括播客、流媒体节目、日本文具等设计产品和技术创新。

title: Uber wants to turn its millions of drivers into a sensor grid for self-driving companies
summary: Uber's CTO revealed a plan to leverage its millions of drivers as a sensor grid for autonomous vehicle companies, describing it as a natural extension of the AV Labs program launched in January. The strategy positions Uber as a data provider for the autonomous vehicle industry while maintaining its existing driver base.
content: Praveen Neppalli Naga, Uber's chief technology officer, revealed the plan in an interview at TechCrunch's StrictlyVC event in San Francisco on Thursday night, describing it as a natural extension of a nascent program the company announced in late January called AV Labs.

zh.title: Uber 想把数百万驾驶员变成自动驾驶公司的传感器网络
zh.summary: Uber 的首席技术官公布了一项计划，利用其数百万驾驶员为自动驾驶公司创建传感器网络，将其描述为 1 月启动的 AV 实验室计划的自然延伸。该策略将 Uber 定位为自动驾驶行业的数据提供商，同时保持其现有的驾驶员基础。
